zh.lifeSummary: 當Percival le Toukere於1358年出生在法國時，他的父親John Percival Toukere29歲，母親Augusta Jane52歲。他和Eolm Ellen Strockes至少有1個兒子。他去世於1384年在英國享年26歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Percival le Toukere于1358年出生在法国时，他的父亲John Percival Toukere29岁，母亲Augusta Jane52岁。他和Eolm Ellen Strockes至少有1个儿子。他去世于1384年在英国享年26岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Percival
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old French versions of the Arthurian legend, where the name is spelled Perceval. According to Chrétien de Troyes (12th century) and Wolfram von Eschenbach ( c.1170–1220 ), Perceval (German Parzifal) was the perfectly pure and innocent knight who alone could succeed in the quest for the Holy Grail (a cup or bowl with supernatural powers, which in medieval legend was identified with the chalice that had received Christ's blood at the Crucifixion). Later versions of the Grail legend assign this role to Sir Galahad . The name Perceval probably represents a drastic remodelling of the Celtic name Peredur, as if from Old French perce(r) ‘pierce’ + val ‘valley’. This may well have been influenced by Percy , which was similarly analysed as a compound of perce(r) ‘pierce’ + haie ‘hedge’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Percival le Toukere was born in 1358, in France, his father, John Percival Toukere, was 29 and his mother, Augusta Jane, was 52. He had at least 1 son with Eolm Ellen Strockes. He died in 1384, in England, at the age of 26, and was buried in Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Percival le Toukere est né en 1358, à France, son père, John Percival Toukere, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Augusta Jane, avait 52 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Eolm Ellen Strockes. Il est décédé en 1384, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 26 ans, et a été enterré à Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Percival le Toukere 1358 in Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Percival Toukere 29 und seine Mutter Augusta Jane 52. Er hatte mit Eolm Ellen Strockes mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1384 im Alter von 26 Jahren in England und wurde in Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Percival le Toukere è nato il nel 1358 nel Francia, il suo padre, John Percival Toukere, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Augusta Jane, aveva 52 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Eolm Ellen Strockes. Morì nel 1384, nel England, all'età di 26 anni, e fu sepolto a Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Percival le Toukereが1358年にフランスで生まれた時，父John Percival Toukereは29歳，母Augusta Janeは52歳であった。彼はEolm Ellen Strockesとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1384年にイギリスで26歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Percival le Toukere(이)가 1358년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Percival Toukere(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Augusta Jane(은)는 52세였다. 그는 Eolm Ellen Strockes(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1384년 영국에서 26세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Percival le Toukere nasceu em 1358, em França, seu pai, John Percival Toukere, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Augusta Jane, tinha 52 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Eolm Ellen Strockes. Ele faleceu em 1384, em Inglaterra, com 26 anos, e foi sepultado em Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Percival le Toukere родился в 1358 г. в местности Франция, его отцу по имени John Percival Toukere было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Augusta Jane, 52. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Eolm Ellen Strockes. Он умер в 1384 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 26 лет и был похоронен в местности сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Percival le Toukere nació en 1358, en Francia, su papá, John Percival Toukere, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Augusta Jane, tenía 52 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Eolm Ellen Strockes. Murió en 1384, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 26 años, y fue sepultado en Somerset, Inglaterra.
